skill.name: zentao-task-planner
skill.description: Plan Zentao tasks and perform Zentao operations including creating tasks, logging work hours, and completing tasks.
skill.summary: AI-powered Zentao task planning and management skill for Claude
skill.author: AriesYB

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Zentao task management integration skill. Static analysis flagged 200 potential issues, but evaluation reveals most are false positives. The reference markdown files show command examples with backtick syntax (not actual code execution). Python system calls like getpass.getuser() and os.path operations are standard path handling, not reconnaissance. Network access is limited to user-configured Zentao instances. Credentials are properly loaded from environment variables with no hardcoded secrets. The skill includes standard safety patterns: preview-only mode with --execute flag, user confirmation required for destructive operations, no arbitrary command injection.

high_findings.title: MD5 Usage in Codebase
high_findings.description: MD5 cryptographic algorithm usage detected in zentao_common.py at multiple locations (lines 32, 38, 41, 42, 44, 47, 68, 443, 765, 772, 787, 812, 911). MD5 is cryptographically weak but appears to be used for non-security purposes like generating unique IDs for form submissions (uuid.uuid4()) rather than password hashing or credential protection.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 usage confirmed but no evidence of cryptographic misuse. Used for form uid generation which is non-security-critical.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Credential Access
medium_findings.description: Skill reads ZENTAO_PASSWORD from environment variables. While this is standard secure practice, users should be warned to never commit .env files containing credentials to version control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard secure credential handling pattern. The .env.example template shows placeholder values, not actual secrets.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to User-Configured Zentao Instance
medium_findings.description: Scripts make HTTP requests to user-specified Zentao server URL. Network access is intentional functionality for task management integration, not data exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is core functionality - connecting to user's own Zentao instance. No external connections except user-configured endpoint.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: External Commands
low_findings.description: Reference markdown files contain command examples using backtick syntax (markdown code formatting), not actual Ruby/shell backtick execution. No command injection vulnerability exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation files contain example commands. No actual code execution with backticks exists in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Code flagged as 'system reconnaissance' uses legitimate Python system calls: getpass.getuser(), os.path.expanduser(), pathlib.Path().resolve(). These are standard operations for path handling and user identification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Python system calls for path handling and environment loading. No suspicious reconnaissance behavior detected.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: Hidden File Access
low_findings.description: .env file access is legitimate configuration loading for Zentao credentials. This is standard practice for credential management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: .env files are standard configuration files. No hidden malicious files are accessed.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: Unicode Escape Sequence
low_findings.description: BOM (Byte Order Mark) ﻿ at line 401 is a UTF-8 encoding marker, not malicious obfuscation. Used for handling Chinese character encoding.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard UTF-8 BOM for Chinese text handling. Legitimate encoding practice for Chinese localization.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: Heuristic flagging of reference markdown files as 'binary/encrypted content' based on entropy. This is likely due to special characters in Chinese text, not actual encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy likely caused by Chinese characters in markdown files, not encrypted or binary content.

content.user_title: Plan and Manage Zentao Tasks with Claude
content.value_statement: Managing Zentao tasks manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill enables Claude to help you plan tasks, calculate workdays, create tasks in bulk, and complete or close tasks automatically based on dates.
actual_capabilities: Query current or next work period with automatic holiday consideration
List tasks from Zentao with multiple view options (active, assigned, finished, closed)
Get task type enums dynamically from Zentao project settings
Create tasks in bulk from standardized tab-separated task plans
Finish tasks automatically based on date ranges or deadlines
Repair incorrect task finish dates by adjusting effort records
Close finished but unclosed tasks in batch
limitations: Requires a self-hosted Zentao instance with valid credentials
Cannot modify or delete existing tasks, only create, finish, and close
Task creation requires pre-existing stories in Zentao

use_cases.title: Weekly Task Planning Session
use_cases.description: At the start of a sprint, use the skill to query upcoming workdays, then help plan and create tasks for the team in bulk from a standardized task table.

use_cases.title: Sprint End Task Cleanup
use_cases.description: At sprint end, use the skill to batch-finish tasks based on their deadlines, then close all finished tasks that are still open.

use_cases.title: Task Finish Date Correction
use_cases.description: When task finish dates were incorrectly recorded, use the repair script to fix effort records and align finish dates with actual deadlines.

prompt_templates.title: List My Active Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao-task-planner skill to show my current active tasks from Zentao.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Next Work Period
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao-task-planner skill to find the next 5 consecutive workdays starting from next Monday.

prompt_templates.title: Preview Batch Task Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao-task-planner skill to preview creating tasks from this task plan: [paste your TSV task table here]

prompt_templates.title: Finish Tasks by Deadline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao-task-planner skill to preview finishing all tasks with deadlines between 2026-05-01 and 2026-05-07.

output_examples.output: Active Tasks: 3 tasks found. Task #123: 'Implement login API' (doing, 4h remaining, deadline: 2026-05-10). Task #124: 'Write unit tests' (wait, 2h estimated, deadline: 2026-05-12). Task #125: 'Code review for PR #45' (doing, 1h remaining, deadline: 2026-05-08).
Workdays found: 5 consecutive workdays starting 2026-05-08. Friday 2026-05-08, Monday 2026-05-11, Tuesday 2026-05-12, Wednesday 2026-05-13, Thursday 2026-05-14.

best_practices: Always preview operations (without --execute) before running to verify correct task selection
Use consistent date formats (YYYY-MM-DD) throughout task planning
Keep task plan files organized by sprint or project for future reference
anti_patterns: Do not create tasks without first confirming story IDs exist in Zentao
Avoid running batch finish operations during active development periods
Do not skip the preview step when operating on multiple tasks

faq.question: What credentials do I need to configure?
faq.answer: You need ZENTAO_BASE_URL (your Zentao instance URL), ZENTAO_ACCOUNT (your login account), and ZENTAO_PASSWORD. Set these in a .env file in the skill directory.

faq.question: How do I create tasks in bulk?
faq.answer: Prepare a tab-separated task plan with columns: name, story ID, task name, task type, estimate hours, start date, deadline. Then use the skill to preview and execute batch creation.

faq.question: What is the preview mode?
faq.answer: All modifying operations (create, finish, close) run in preview mode by default. Add --execute flag only after reviewing the preview to confirm the changes are correct.

faq.question: Can this skill delete tasks?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot delete tasks. It can only create new tasks, finish existing tasks, and close finished tasks.

faq.question: How are workdays calculated?
faq.answer: The skill uses the chinese-calendar library to automatically exclude weekends and Chinese public holidays when calculating consecutive workdays.

faq.question: What if task creation fails?
faq.answer: The script validates all task data before creation and reports errors line by line. Failed tasks are skipped while successful ones are created.
